findings.title: Opened file is never closed
findings.description: os.Open returns an *os.File that is never closed on any return path, leaking a file descriptor each time ReadAll is called and eventually exhausting the process file-descriptor limit.
findings.suggestion: Add defer f.Close() immediately after the err != nil check so the file is released on every return path.

findings.title: ReadAll truncates files past 1024 bytes
findings.description: The function reads a single time into a fixed 1024-byte buffer and returns buf[:n], so any file larger than 1024 bytes is silently truncated despite the ReadAll name.
findings.suggestion: Read the whole file: return io.ReadAll(f) (with defer f.Close()).
